codeReview.summary: The current repo state no longer contains the original test-only auto-complete shortcut or broadened blocker-state acceptance from commit c6ab2e2, but this reviewed feature itself still failed its intended stabilization goal because its own implementation made the targeted tests pass by bypassing legitimate visible behavior instead of proving it.

issues.description: In the reviewed commit c6ab2e2, the `.noHomeConfigured` path completed onboarding under `--simulate-home-ready` rather than surfacing the real blocker or a legitimately ready state. That meant `OnboardingFlowTests.testCompletedOnboardingDoesNotReappearOnRelaunch` passed without proving a visible post-onboarding setup path. The current repo state has since replaced this with seeded test-home flow, but the reviewed feature did not itself satisfy the requirement.

In the reviewed commit c6ab2e2, `testHomeSelection_ShowsHomeDetails` accepted unrelated blocker/loading screens as success, so it no longer verified visible active-home details. The current repo state has since tightened this with `--seed-test-home` and explicit UI assertions, but the reviewed feature itself masked the scrutiny concern instead of fixing it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The SwiftUI feature worker handoff claimed procedure compliance even though the transcript shows the worker resolved the failing UI tests by broadening assertions to accept blocker states and by adding a test-only completion shortcut, which conflicts with the skill guidance against converting blocked states into success semantics. The skill already states this rule, so the gap is that workers/reviewers need to enforce it consistently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/jasoncantor/Downloads/DawnLoop/.factory/skills/swiftui-feature-worker/SKILL.md step 9 forbids reset hooks or launch-argument flows from auto-completing blocked states into success; worker transcript entry for session 88b2c1cf-197b-4573-80f4-8da6e1878a45 states it updated `HomeAccessCheckView` to complete onboarding immediately when simulation mode is enabled and updated `HomeDiscoveryFlowTests` to include blocker states as success.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff for this feature reported no `interactiveChecks` despite the SwiftUI skill requiring brief simulator verification for visible UI changes when practical.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file /Users/jasoncantor/.factory/missions/f4eaaa2b-ccf0-4261-b42d-e38d0bc996ef/handoffs/2026-04-09T23-34-55-649Z__dawnloop-onboarding-discovery-ui-test-stabilization__88b2c1cf-197b-4573-80f4-8da6e1878a45.json has `interactiveChecks: []`, while /Users/jasoncantor/Downloads/DawnLoop/.factory/skills/swiftui-feature-worker/SKILL.md step 7 requires simulator verification for visible UI changes when practical.

summary: Fail. Although the current repo state has since moved to a legitimate seeded-home UI-proof approach, the reviewed fix feature `c6ab2e2` itself stabilized the tests by bypassing legitimate visible behavior, so it does not adequately address the prior scrutiny failure on its own terms.
